title: Community Outreach Teams (CREST)
text:
Community Outreach Teams (CREST) - SupportGoWhere
Community Outreach Teams (CREST)
Share link
Listens to your concerns and provides support such as therapy, as your in-person First Stop for Mental Health.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Free
Referral required
Collapse all sections
About this support

You can approach over 100 community mental health teams in the community islandwide such as CREST, providing the following support:

Promote early recognition of signs and symptoms of mental health conditions and/or dementia. 

 Improve mental health conditions and/or dementia literacy by organising targeted outreach events and activities to provide mental health and/or dementia information and education to residents and caregivers in the region.

Provide psychosocial intervention such as techniques to reduce the risk of escalation of mental health needs. 

Provide collaborative and coordinated care for clients and caregivers through a case management approach.

Work in partnership with health and community care partners and key stakeholders to co-manage and support persons with mental health conditions and dementia in the community.

 

These community mental health teams are developed under the Community Mental Health Masterplan driven by the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC), together with the Ministry of Health (MOH). The teams are fully funded by the government and there is no out-of-pocket cost.

Who is this for?

Any individual who is:

At risk of mental health conditions and/or dementia, and their caregivers

Aged 18 and above

What to expect?

Applicants may need to fulfil the services’ in-take criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any.

How to apply?

Call or email the providers to make an appointment. Visit AIC’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

If you are already seeing a General Practitioner (GP) or Polyclinic doctor, you may approach them for support.

Information last updated on 24 Feb 2026
